title: salix × blyttiana
intro: Salix × blyttiana, also known as Blytt's willow or Blytt's alder willow, is a hybrid plant of the Salix genus, derived from the cross between Salix alba and Salix myrsinifolia. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly due to the presence of salicin, a compound that is also responsible for the anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects of white willow bark. The health benefits associated with Salix × blyttiana include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate headaches and joint pain, and lower inflammation. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or salves, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments.
